consistency.level_school_coherence: The level (3) is appropriate for a utility transmutation spell like Water Breathing.
consistency.duration_power_balance: A duration of 24 hours is excellent for a 3rd-level spell, offering significant utility.
consistency.component_justification: Verbal, Somatic, and Material components (a reed/straw) are standard and fit the theme of enabling aquatic respiration.

quality.completeness: All standard fields (slug, name, level, school, casting_time, range, components, duration, description, classes) are present.
quality.clarity: The description is clear: grants underwater breathing to up to 10 creatures, and they keep normal respiration.
quality.flavor: The flavor aligns well with Transmutation magic that alters biological functions.

mechanics.target_and_count: Targeting 'up to ten willing creatures' within range is a strong group utility feature.
mechanics.interaction_with_normal_respiration: The clause 'Affected creatures also retain their normal mode of respiration' is crucial, ensuring the spell doesn't cause asphyxiation when leaving water, which is good design.
mechanics.ritual_status: Marked as a ritual (true), which enhances its utility, allowing casters to prepare long-term explorations without expending a spell slot.
